title: malva scaberrima
intro: Malva scaberrima, also known as the rough mallow or scabrous mallow, is a perennial plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to southern Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Malva scaberrima include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating menstrual cramps, among others. Malva scaberrima is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
